prohibited_paths: profile migration code that handles real user data
preconditions: profile, Space, session, snapshot, and migration terms are defined consistently
checked no-claim schema-package template remains current

checked no-claim profile/session readiness-review template remains current and cannot be cited for data-loss safety, user-data handling readiness, production profile-format approval, release-path approval, or implementation evidence
privacy and downgrade behavior are treated as first-class requirements
no real user profile data is used as a fixture
queue status remains proposed; this specified manifest is non-executable
a named owner and named independent reviewer replace the role placeholders before this manifest can become ready
acceptance_criteria: versioned schema proposals cover profile, Space, session, snapshot, and migration records
checked no-claim profile/session format inventory is treated as planning evidence until executable schemas and migration tests exist

first executable schema package starts from the checked no-claim schema-package template and replaces template-only fields with real schema, migration-test, fault-test, fixture-policy, and owner-review evidence

first profile/session readiness review starts from the checked no-claim profile/session readiness-review template and replaces template-only null schema_package, reference_platform, fixture_policy, owner_reviewer, independent_reviewer, and privacy_reviewer fields with owner-reviewed evidence beyond the template
disk-full, power-loss, corruption, downgrade, export, deletion, private-session, crash-recovery, protected-work, privacy, and data-loss behavior are specified
crash recovery and user-work preservation rules are linked to product requirements and risks
unsupported sync, credential storage, real-profile migration, user-data handling, and production profile-format behavior remains explicit
negative_tests: schemas cannot silently discard unsaved or protected work
private-session data cannot leak into normal profile fixtures
downgrade or corrupt input cannot be treated as successful migration
real user profile data cannot be used as a fixture before owner-approved privacy and migration review

checked no-claim schema-package template cannot be cited as executable schema approval, data-loss safety evidence, user-data handling readiness, production profile-format evidence, or real-profile migration approval

checked no-claim profile/session readiness-review template cannot be cited as data-loss safety, user-data handling readiness, production profile-format approval, release-path approval, sync support, credential-storage support, or implementation evidence

rollback.strategy: Revert schema proposals and readiness wording if privacy, recovery, or downgrade behavior cannot be specified.
